headline: What Just Happened In LoL Esports World Cup Playoffs?
dek: Favorites are falling and underdogs are rising in a shake-up that's changing everything for the LoL Esports World Cup 2026.
body_markdown:
The League of Legends Esports World Cup 2026 playoffs are currently delivering a masterclass in unpredictability, as long-shot teams pull off stunning victories and established powerhouses face unexpected eliminations. This year's tournament is rewriting the script, reminding everyone that in esports, nothing is ever truly guaranteed.

Leading the charge from the West, LEC team Karmine Corp (KCorp) secured a remarkable 2-1 win over AG.AL. This victory not only propelled KCorp further into the Knockout Stages but also marked them as the sole Western team to survive this cutthroat phase, an outcome that certainly defied many expectations.

Meanwhile, the East saw its own set of seismic shifts. Reigning LoL World Champions T1 delivered a clinical performance, sweeping MSI champions Hanwha Life Esports (HLE) with a decisive 2-0 victory, sending HLE packing. In another massive upset, DPlus Kia (DK) executed what has been described as a "spectacular comeback," besting First Stand champions Bilibili Gaming (BLG) 2-1. Both BLG and HLE were widely considered tournament favorites on Polymarket, making their early exits all the more impactful.

Amidst this flurry of upsets, one team has remained an unyielding force: reigning EWC champions Gen.G. They have demonstrated an iron grip on the competition, defeating JD Gaming 2-0 on July 17, 2026, and notably, have yet to drop a single game throughout the tournament. Their flawless run sets a formidable benchmark for the teams still in contention.

Adding another layer of intrigue to DPlus Kia's impressive run are reports that their players are currently facing significant off-field pressure, reportedly owed a month's wages. Despite these potential distractions, DK continues to perform at an elite level, battling for a share of the substantial $2 million prize pool.

With favorites tumbling and underdogs rising, the competitive landscape of the LoL Esports World Cup 2026 is constantly shifting. The remaining matches promise to be a captivating display of skill, resilience, and the relentless pursuit of supremacy.

sources.title: LoL EWC 2026 Top Four Confirmed with Shock KCorp and DPlus Wins - Insider Gaming
